text_segment: Simple interest is calculated only on the original amount of money. If you borrow $100 at 10% interest for 2 years, you pay $20 in interest. The formula is principal times rate times time.

text_segment: Compound interest is interest paid on top of interest you already earned. If you earn 4% on $100, you have $104. Next year you earn 4% on $104, giving you $108.16. Your money grows faster over time.
